sentences.text: Hello!
Hi, how are you?
Good, how you doing?
Good.
How's your day going so far?
It's going!
How's yours?
It is also going.
I feel like this is, like, a very long, short week.
Like, I feel like it was supposed to feel shorter than this, but it feels very…
Thank you.
Agreed.
I also agree.
We're almost there, it's Friday Eve.
It is.
I don't know who else is joining, I sent it to a few people, but I see that you're recording, so it might be just us, and then I can send it out to everyone after the call.
Sounds good.
Yeah, it sounded like Taylor was interested in seeing the product as well, so, I can send her the recording, or we could always jump on another call, too.
Okay.
Thank you.
Sarah, I don't expect you to remember, but we met very briefly at your marketing conference in Vegas.
Yes.
No, it's okay if you don't remember, there's a lot of people there.
Nice to see you again!
Good to see you again.
Good to see you again.
It's a long time ago, there was a lot of people there.
Yeah.
I'm gonna try to remember where you were based.
I think you were in, like, were you in the West Coast?
Yes?
That's… you weren't one of the Seattle crew, were you?
No, I'm San Diego.
So…
I thought you were gonna have it, though, with this… with the start of it.
I was gonna be very impressed.

It was West Coast, I couldn't remember, because there's… you guys kind of broke it up by, like, region, so a group of… everybody from the Pacific Northwest would come through, and then you'd have some Southern California folks and Texas folks, so…
Yeah.
Don't remember which cohort you were with.
Yeah.
Well, still a good, great memory.
Well, I will be making it to that conference next year.
I was very sad.
I was on vacation that week.
Oh, that's not bad.
I got the invite, and I was really bummed, but… next year.
Awesome.
Well, well, I guess we can just start with… oh, it looks like we had one drop off.
Just wanted to start with how familiar you are with the product.
So, I know it sounds like this was kind of passed your way internally, and I know we've never gone through a demo, so I just wanted.
Yeah.
how much you know about the product, how familiar you are.
If you want me to kind of run through a demo, I can do that as well.
Yeah, not familiar, really, at all.
I've heard things from other people that have, used it, but I don't really know the ins and outs of it.
So, yeah, I would love all the info.
Cool.
Yeah, well, I can jump in and give a demo of the product.
In short, it's an automated tool for your Google Business profiles.
So, we're automating different areas throughout the profile, and I have, you're working with Alton Mill District, right?
Yeah, so I am temporary on this property.
There's, we just hired a new regional marketing manager who's actually, on the East… coast.
I've never been there, so I'm not the best person.
So I was just kind of filling in until they found someone new.

So, Catherine is her name, she'll be kind of really starting in the next few weeks, but I wanted to obviously learn a bit more, not only for this property, but also, because I am you know, I oversee or help with key clients across multiple… who have properties across multiple regions, so if this is something that would benefit one of their portfolios, I would love to speak to it.
Yeah.
So are you more aligned, with different ownership groups, or.
Yeah.
Or both.
No, so I am not a… I am not… I don't have a particular region.

I work with many of our key clients, our institutional clients, who have properties across multiple regions, so they don't have to talk to, you know, 5, 6, 7 different regional marketing managers, about higher, like.
higher initiatives, or rollouts, you know, I help with a lot of rollouts that are touching multiple regions.
So, if this is something that works well.
you know, something that I can recommend, to those clients to, you know, implement on… across multiple properties.
Yeah.
You said you heard of it, Sarah.
Did you hear?
You didn't express if it was, like, good hearing or bad hearing?
Yeah, yeah, no, good.
That's, no, definitely good stuff.
That's why, you know, we wanted to try it out at Alton Mill District, because it is, we rebranded completely, well, from Alexin to Alton, so, you know, this, along with, kind of, what was it?
Another SEO platform, to kind of help with that new brand and to get our name out there.
We think that will be super beneficial, so… from what I hear, you guys have definitely helped other communities, so yeah, just trying to learn a bit more.
Yeah, makes sense.
We, we originally met with, Allie Schwab, and, she had started rolling this out, and Sherry Killingsworth.

So it was the two that we met with them, and they rolled out, I think it was 6 properties, and now I've been just getting emails for property… But it's been from such different people, and, like, different titles, so I've been very curious how this is being communicated internally, and…
Pretty much Allie.
Okay, that makes sense.
Every time we're on a call, which is like… I talk with her more than maybe other regional marketing managers.
Yes.
But we do have monthly calls, and… I want to say she's spoken about this two-plus times on different calls, so we're like, okay, let's get it rolled out.
Awesome.
We're talking with, I think, tomorrow, Maria and a couple people as well.
Obviously, has expressed interest in this product, too, and.
Yeah, I'm glad Allie's saying great things.
Yeah.

Well, I'll jump in and share, just reasons why companies like F5 have rolled us out, use cases for what they're seeing with this product, and then I'll jump in and go through Alton Mill District, since that's the property that, you're most familiar with.
Perfect.
Sarah, real quick, Kylie, sorry, I have one more question.
Sarah, we've gotten a lot of our customers saying that they're… The ownership groups are getting more and more interested in what's going on in Google.
Are you seeing that in your world, too, or how are you… how are your ownership and clients thinking about this?
Are you talking about Google, like, the AI, or just Google in general?
All things organic, Google, profile, AI overviews, what we can do to be found on Google, like…
all… that question comes up a lot, and… but really, I mean, I would say right at this very moment, AI is the big… the big question that everyone is talking about.
So, chat GPT… Google's, AI, all of that.
Yeah, we've heard that.
Yeah, it's the hot topic.
What are you seeing with ChatGPT?
Like, how are you answering those questions from clients who are like, hey, how are we found on ChatGPT?

Well, that's an interesting question, because we don't really have an answer, but we are trying to, obviously, increase our copy, or make it more robust, and just refresh it, like, on the websites and everything, and then, obviously, blogs are now a big thing again.
Additionally with that, it's, it's funny, because you… we'll look at Google Analytics, and then… Recently, we've seen, like, a lot of… searches come from ChatGPT.
So we're just trying, you know, it's new-ish, so we're still trying to figure that out ourselves.
What do you… what do you… how do you know that searches are coming from ChatGBT?
It says it in Google Analytics.
It does, okay, I know that.
I know that, I know that the ChatGPT does crawl Google.
Like, they kind of can be intertwined.
And we've also heard, like, blogs are a thing again.
Yeah, yep.
I'm sure you're focused on FAQs as well, everybody loves.
Yes.
So…
yet.
I think you're right, though.
A lot of… a lot of folks are like.
how can we be found on ChatGPT, and we don't really know all the answers yet.
I think it's still so new.
We don't… it's kind of a black box a little bit, but we know we should be doing blogs and FAQs and stuff like that, so…
Yeah, and it's so interesting.

I was on with one of our digital suppliers yesterday, just talking about, you know, our paid search, and they're like, across the board, it's down, because people are now searching just in ChatGPT, like, best places to live in San Diego with a pool, by the beach, you know, like, very specific, and they'll get, you know, a handful of replies, and that's what they'll go off of, you know?
So, it's been a huge change in the last, you know, year, really.
And I love those AI overviews on Google.
Yeah.
Which is great, which makes it hard, though, for paid search.
Like, if you're doing.
Yeah.
Google and AI overviews is what is being delivered, but…
Hmm.
I don't know if he went to AIM, but Google's starting to do, advertising space within the paid over… within the AI over.
Yeah.
I think…
Like, your digital providers will have to… and maybe already doing that, will have to take that into consideration, because…
when those AI overviews become more and more, like, predominant, it's gonna make paid search Different, and possibly a little less effective.

Yeah, we were at our marketing conference in Arizona, like, maybe 3 weeks ago now, maybe a month ago, and all of our, you know, suppliers were there, and that was definitely something… that was a hot topic there, too.
So, it's… it's something everyone's trying to figure out, for sure.
Well, everything you're gonna see today, like Kylie said, pointed at organic Google approach with profiles.
everything we're reading from Google is saying that your up-to-date profile will be factored in AI overviews and AI search, so they're kind of intertwined organically.
And so what we'll show you today is just, hey, the more data and information that you have on your profiles, it only helps you in the age of AI search.
And delivering structured, fresh, business-specific content.
Every single day.
Perfect.

So on the topic of AI overviews, so that's a big piece of this, is we know that Google is actually using the content that lives on your Google profiles as one of the sources for answering AI overviews.
And they also… Google tells us that the Google Business Profile itself has the highest influence overall on your local SEO.

So, that just speaks to the importance of having a fully filled out and optimized profile, pushing amenity keywords to your profile, having live, real-time pricing and availability on your profile, and then, like Patrick said, that FAQ-type content.
So, that's what this product solves.
is getting all of that information there, so when Google's looking for that information, especially in those AI overviews, they have that information right on your profile.
Okay, perfect.

We know that Google also rewards on a few different things when it comes to the content on your profile, is they're looking to make sure that… to make sure the content is relevant to your business, so in our case, property.
So, property-specific content's huge here.
And then they also look at the frequency and the uniqueness of that content.
So, everything that we've built is fit to fit what Google's looking for, to increase exposure for your properties, get more eyes on your properties, get more traffic to your website.
So, how this works is we built an automated tool that syndicates directly from your feed to your profiles.
So we have direct syndications to all of the major property management softwares, and that's the source of truth for all of the information we're pulling.

So, amenity-based information, photos, pricing availability, all of that comes from your feed, and then we layer on top of that an AI tool that creates the content based on what we're getting from the feed.

So, when we met with the team a couple months back, a few things that they told us was, a reason that they rolled us out, was they were looking for a tool to expand on the features that they had for the Google Business tool they were using before.
And then in addition to that, there was a lot of team members that were manually posting.
So, obviously, that's time-consuming, it takes a lot of time to create content, schedule content.
So they looked at us for the automation piece to save time, and then on top of that, like I was mentioning, to further optimize the profile, and get more eyes on your profiles.

So, I guess I know that we're just talking about the first property that you've been working with, but how are you handling posting or optimizing of your profiles right now outside of this one property?
Great question.

I don't really… I'm not really… focused on one particular property, so I can't speak to how most people are doing it, I'm really just leaning in on this one, so, I don't know what the other RMMs are doing.
Yeah.
So, when you're talking with the ownership groups that you're working with, do you… are you guys talking about the Google profiles and how to get them more optimized?
Not often, no, that's not really a topic, other than, you know, making sure that they're claimed.
You know, we have photos on there, you know, like you said, updated.
For example, we noticed, you know, a few had really outdated floor plans showing, and so it's like, okay, make sure that they're updated, you know, just trying to audit those.
And honestly, the on-site team should be doing that every month with their audits they do.
They should be doing their digital audits, called a POP5.
So they should be reviewing those, making sure that all the information is accurate, it's pulling through.
So it's really kind of something that the on-site teams should be doing.
Obviously, the regional marketing managers or field marketing specialists should be looking at that as well, but probably not as frequently as the on-site teams.
Would those audits mostly consist of ensuring that, like, phone numbers and addresses and hours are correct?
Yes.
Okay.
Yeah, so not nearly as deep as probably they should be.
Yeah.
Did you call that a pop 5?
Was that called a POP 5?
Yes.
Right, I've not heard… that's gotta be an internal term, right?
Oh yeah, it's our… it's our internal.
Yeah, we do everything with 5.
I was gonna say, I've not heard of a pot pie before.
Nice.
Yep.
So, ensuring that that information's fully filled out is definitely, like, the first step.
So, you have to make sure the properties are claimed and you have the profile claimed.
Where our tool does is takes it a step further and layers on top of ensuring that that basic information is correct.
Our pro… our tool is actually optimizing different areas throughout the profile.

So, with that syndication to your feed, we are automating daily posts, we're automating live pricing and availability to the products feed, we're pulling concession data directly from your feed to your profiles, and then we're automating Q&A.
So, a little bit different.
That's amazing.
Yeah.
Much more, robust than what the teams are looking at, for sure.
up.
Let me hop over to this property to give you an example of what it's looking like.
So, this property's only been live since… it went live August 31st, so we have a few days of posts that have loaded in, but what we're doing here is it's a unique post every day.
like I mentioned before, it's all automated, content pulling from your feed.
And then every day, our tool is creating content to match that photo.
So, a bit more of, like, an organic SEO play here to push amenity keywords, make sure that your profile is being consistently updated, and then getting information in front of prospects as well.
Consistency's key here.

I mean, a lot of companies, a lot of portfolios of your size and scale, it's just really hard to rely on on-site teams to consistently do this, so… perfect kind of area to layer in AI to make sure you're getting relevant business content every single day.
Yeah, definitely.
The next place that we're automating is the products feed, so I'll say this is the piece that got Ali the most excited.
Yep.
The, the pricing and availability feed.
Yeah, that's amazing.
So, this is reflecting real-time pricing and availability at the floor plan level, and then it's updated every day.
So, every day, our system goes in and it pings, I think this property was Rent Cafe.
Four times a day, we're going into paying what pricing and availability looks like right now, and then updating this to reflect that.
Amazing.
So we do have a new feature that we just rolled out that is relevant to the products feed, and it is pulling in amenities.
So I want to show an example of this, because this is a newer feature, and it's an opt-in, so I wanted to ask you guys today if this is something that you wanted live on this property.
So we're pulling in unit-level amenities and community-level amenities right from the feature profile with photos.
Yeah, is this, included?
Yep, yep.
No additional fee?
Yep, no.
Yeah, why not?
Yep.
Yeah, we've had a lot of excitement around this, because there is no other automated tool that can pull these in, these tiles into the products feed.
Yeah.
So, we'll get this turned on.
It's usually pretty quick, takes, like, a day.
Okay.
So, we'll confirm and send it over when it's live.
Awesome.
Just one more place we can be pushing amenity keywords into your.
profile.
So…
Yeah.
And then the next area is within that Q&A.
So let me show you what this looks like.
What we're doing here is we're automating, the Q&A questions.
So we're posting and answering some of the most frequently asked Q&A questions.

We talked about it a little bit earlier, but we know AI overviews really like FAQ type of content, so a lot of customers have been saying that they're focusing on getting this live on their websites, so this is the same thing, just getting it live on your Google profile as well.
Perfect.
If we notice, that there's…
information that's not entirely accurate, how would we go about changing that?
Yep, yeah, you can message, myself and Morgan.
Okay.
So, shoot us an email, we'll get it updated.
Perfect.
I see everything here, Sarah.
It's updated.
It says 19 hours ago, so it'll reflect today.
Like, the… the question itself is placed and then answered.
And refreshed every day.
We do that because one of the questions is about availability, and that changes often, so we want to make sure these are accurate.
If, for whatever reason.
you unmarked pool as an amenity in Rent Cafe, the next day, it would say, like, that question would be taken down.
That's how live this is.
Got it, okay.
It's every single day, refreshed and updated.
Amazing.
We're using this to highlight amenities that your property does have, so if this property didn't have a pool, we wouldn't be asking that question.
And then same thing with availability.
As soon as your two bedrooms are fully leased, we won't be highlighting that you don't have that floor plan type available.
Okay.
So, this is a live feed, just like everything else you've seen.
Any questions on anything we've gone over so far?
No, that is great.
One more feature I wanted to highlight within the posts is concessions.
So, we're only a couple days into the posts, but, if we… as long as your property has concessions advertised in the feed, one of those posts per week will be highlighting that concession.
Okay.
So, pulling that directly over from the feed to your profile.
So, we know a lot of time and thought goes into creating concessions, so this is just one more place we can be advertising that for you guys.
Awesome.
Cool.
Yeah.
Wanted to include this here.
We've got about… we just hit 25 properties live with Avenue 5.
So, your list in here, but this is what the list of properties we have live right now is.
Our time to go live's been about 5 days, so from the time we get the feed to having everything all live on your profile, it's about 5 business days.
Okay.
So, I know Wesley on Broadway, for example, is rebranding.
Yep.
So how does that work?
Do we just say, hey.
This is rebranding, and then everything… not everything, but, like, the name and everything changes.
Yep.
Automatically?
Okay.
Yeah, so because we're pulling it in from the feed, we'll be pulling… as soon as that property name is changed in the feed, all of our posts will be changed as well.
You can obviously always ping us and give us a heads up on it so we can make sure we're keeping an eye out for it.
But yeah, as long as it's changed in the feed, it'll also change in our posts.
Perfect.
Yep.
Same thing with photos, too.

So photos get updated from, like, a remodel, we'll be pulling them directly over from the feed, but if you know a remodel's coming, or you have new photography, and you want to make sure we're getting it right away, you can always send us, like, a Dropbox link as well, and we can layer those photos on top of what we're pulling from the feed.
Okay, and I'm sure you're gonna go over this, reporting.
Yep.
Okay, great.

I'm thinking we have a LA property that's gonna be rebranding at the end of the year, so they're not doing great right now, so I'm thinking we could probably use you guys, but, I wanna… I know that the client's gonna ask about that, so…
Yeah.
So, the reporting… I threw in a report in here that just shows the averages.
So, this is an average of the increases in traffic that we're seeing so far since going live.
So, this is the first group of properties, I think this is the first 6 properties, for the first 3 months.
So we pulled the first 3 months compared to the same time last year to account for seasonality.
And this shows change in impressions, change in website actions, map impressions.
So, if, if you can see this.
So 15% increase in website clicks.
4% increase in impressions.
And then driving directions up 21, phone actions.
Is this the type of reporting you're… you were looking for?
Yeah, I think so.
This would be great, and obviously, as, you know.

we look at Alton Mill District, even though it's a completely different, region and area, it's still… it's still good to share that information with the client, you know, for a specific property that we've recently rolled out on.
Yep.
Yeah, we can also break this down by property, too.

So if you wanted to just see one for a property, or we eventually get to where we have, like, a full ownership group on, we can pull whatever list of properties you're looking for, for whatever date range.
And is this something I can log into, or is this a request that I would just shoot you an email about?
The reporting's currently not a self-serve, so you can ping us, and we'll get it to you within the same day.
Okay.
Cool, anything else I'm missing, Patrick Morgan?
I don't think so.
Alright.
Pretty easy to get rolling, Sarah, so as you have your client.
conversations, like… like Kylie said, it gets live in 5… 5 days.
best part is, even if it's, like, a new property that you take over, in a day, you can have everything about this property on your Google Business profile.
So, it's really fast, and… delivers consistent property-specific information, which is so key for Google.
Awesome.
No, this is great.
I'm excited to see how… how it works and performs and everything, and hopefully your conversation with Maria goes well tomorrow, and, you know, we can get more properties going on this.
We hope so as well!
Awesome.
Thank you so much.
This is per… and you're able to send this to me after the call?
Yeah.
Cool.
Yep, yeah, I can send this deck over, and then obviously just let us know if you have any questions.
We'll get that amenities feed live, too, on this one property, and then we'll confirm as soon as that's done.
Awesome!
Thank you so much!
Thank you.
Thanks.
Sarah.
I appreciate your time.
Thanks, buddy.
Alright, see ya.
Bye.
